vocab.meaning: nảy mầm, mọc lên nhanh chóng
vocab.example: It’s incredibly rewarding to see the tiny seedlings finally sprout up after weeks of patient watering.

vocab.meaning: tỉa bớt (cây con) để có không gian phát triển
vocab.example: If the carrots are planted too closely together, you need to thin them out so the roots have room to expand.

vocab.meaning: cắt tỉa bớt (cành, lá) để cây mọc khỏe hơn
vocab.example: To encourage more flowers next season, you should prune back the dead branches during the winter months.

vocab.meaning: tươi tỉnh trở lại, hưng phấn hơn (sau khi được tưới nước)
vocab.example: My peace lily was looking quite wilted, but it quickly perked up after I gave it a good soak.

vocab.meaning: chuyển cây sang chậu lớn hơn
vocab.example: Once the roots start emerging from the bottom of the container, it's definitely time to pot the plant on.

vocab.meaning: xới đất, lật đất (để chuẩn bị gieo trồng)
vocab.example: Before sowing any seeds, you must dig over the soil to ensure it's well-aerated and free of weeds.

vocab.meaning: khô cạn, mất nước (đất hoặc cây)
vocab.example: Terracotta pots tend to dry out much faster than plastic ones, so you have to keep a close eye on the moisture levels.

vocab.meaning: bón phân, cung cấp dưỡng chất dồi dào
vocab.example: During the growing season, I make sure to feed up my tomato plants with organic liquid fertilizer every fortnight.
It usually takes about ten days for the cuttings to take root and show signs of new growth.

vocab.meaning: héo đi/lụi đi (thường là phần trên mặt đất vào mùa đông)
vocab.example: Many perennials will die back to the ground in late autumn, only to re-emerge stronger in the spring.
